sections.paragraph:
στενάζω [στένω ‘moan, groan’] ‘make a deep inarticulate moaning sound’, groan, sigh

—a. in response to a disturbing stimulus Mk 7:34; Ro 8:23; 2 Cor 5:2, 4; w. a feeling of being imposed on Hb 13:17.

—b. metaph. ext., expressing dissatisfaction in a relationship, groan, complain (against) Js 5:9.
